feedback.text: One area I believe the Digital Fairness Act should cover more clearly is digital gamesboth new purchases and games people already own. Today, it's far too common for players to lose access to something they paid for, simply because a company decides to shut down servers. A good example is The Crew, which became unplayable even for those who bought it. That kind of loss shouldnt be acceptable in a fair digital market. If someone buys a game, they should be able to keep playing it. The Act should make sure that kind of basic consumer right is protected, especially in such a big and fast-growing part of the digital economy.
